title: Jelly Bottle Lens Refraction Lab
description: A soft-body water bottle sits on a table, wobbling like gelatin when poked. The user squeezes, stretches, and deforms the jiggly bottle to aim refracted light beams through floating sunglasses lenses, unlocking color channels to fill the bottle with prismatic liquid.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a soft-body deformation lab. Load WaterBottle.glb centered on a platform and SunglassesKhronos.glb floating above-right. Setup: PerspectiveCamera at (0,1.5,3) looking at origin, OrbitControls enabled, HDR-style lighting with a DirectionalLight (warm white, intensity 2) plus AmbientLight (0.3). Add a subtle gradient background. Soft-body simulation: Represent the water bottle mesh vertices with a spring-mass system. Store original vertex positions. On each frame, apply spring forces pulling vertices toward rest positions with damping (stiffness 0.4, damping 0.15). When the user clicks and drags on the bottle, displace nearby vertices radially outward based on pointer proximity (radius 0.15), creating a squishy deformation that jiggles back. Interactions (7 steps with validation): Step 1 - Click the bottle to activate it (validate: bottle glows softly on selection). Step 2 - Drag downward on the bottle to squeeze it shorter (validate: bounding box height decreases by >15%). Step 3 - Drag outward to bulge the bottle sideways (validate: bounding box width increases >20%). Step 4 - Pinch gesture or shift+drag to stretch the bottle upward (validate: height exceeds 130% of original). Step 5 - Rotate the sunglasses by clicking them and dragging to align lenses with the directional light path (validate: angle within 10 degrees of light direction). Step 6 - Deform the bottle into a concave lens shape so a visible light ray (rendered as a bright line using RaycasterHelper or a thin cylinder) passes through both sunglasses lenses and hits the bottle (validate: ray intersection detected on bottle mesh). Step 7 - Once the light hits the deformed bottle, animate prismatic liquid filling from bottom up using a gradient shader on the bottle interior (validate: fill level reaches 100%). Display an HTML overlay panel showing current step, deformation percentage, and a reset button. Each completed step triggers a subtle particle burst. Use requestAnimationFrame for the spring simulation update loop. Ensure all vertex manipulations use geometry.attributes.position with needsUpdate flag.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bottleHeight: number,         // current bounding box height of the deformed bottle in world units
  bottleWidth: number,         // current bounding box width (max of x/z dimensions) of the deformed bottle
  bottleSelected: boolean,         // true when bottle is clicked and active for deformation
  totalDeformationEnergy: number,         // accumulated spring potential energy from vertex displacements
  refractionBeamActive: boolean,         // true when light beam is passing through bottle geometry
  lensesAligned: boolean,         // true when sunglasses lenses intersect the refraction beam path
  colorChannelsUnlocked: number,         // count of RGB channels unlocked (0-3) by successful beam-lens alignments
  bottleFluidLevel: number,         // fill percentage of prismatic liquid inside bottle (0.0 to 1.0)
  vertexDisplacementMax: number,         // maximum vertex displacement magnitude from rest position in current frame
  springOscillationFrequency: number,         // dominant frequency of bottle jiggle oscillation in Hz
  userInteractionCount: number,         // total number of click-drag deformation interactions performed
  beamRefractionAngle: number,         // current refraction angle in degrees when beam passes through bottle
}
